corrections 1 some people who know more than me have said that when sam says query he does indeed mean the query and all of the subsequent work done by the models as part of that one query this is probably true but it's also the kind of thing that would be cleared up if openai shared information rather than just talking points 2 some people have said that when i say query i say it weird i did not realize this i put it down to having learned that sql is pronounced sequel and that sequel queery sounds silly i have not done a sql query since 2005 so i'm sure you all are right

the oregon state legislature is considering bills that would have these data centers pay their own electric bills what a novel idea

as someone who is a power quality engineer specifically dealing with harmonic distortions its not just data centers literally almost every major factory produces it the difference is datacenters are almost entirely electronic based which makes harmonics way more easy to occur i would love to answer any questions if i can

our city just blocked one of these the home owners in the area signed a contract when they built in the area that it would be medical and regular businesses they tried to push this through by only giving the residences a few days notice of a meeting to allow the build they all showed up and voted it down which we are all thankful for

is there anything in america that corporations aren't making worse

in michigan there is a data center that has made headlines for causing thousands of residents to lose water service and causing sewers to back up into their basements and crawlspaces right next door is a factory with twice the water usage that has been there for 75 years and has done none of this the difference is that the data center is not classified as industrial water and is allowed to pull from the same pipes as those homes while the factory has a dedicated blue pipe from the municipal supply a huge buffer tank to protect that line from rapid pressure changes and a dedicated collection sump and drainage line back to the river this is all very expensive but it means they can use more water than every home in the city and never so much as sputter a faucet the data center has none of that it just has hoses connected to spigots fed by the pipes that were in the building way back when it was an american bell switch rack and drains going down into the flood sump in the basement and feeding into the sewers
the power grid needs to be talked about more than the water its not just the power usage but how unprepared the grid infrastructure is for the increase in demand

hey data center mechanical engineer here i litterally design the cooling for data centers artificial intelligence chips live there while evaporative cooling is used the majority are closed loop cooling it uses water but just as a way to transfer heat to mechanical chillers this does however use a lot of power as a trade off so yeah know still using water to generate that power

as a water resource engineer and manager of a medium sized water utility who sends about 10 of our municipal water to a large data center hank you actually did a fantastic job of explaining into explaining some really advanced concepts in water management you might not have degrees in water management but your research and deduction skills are extraordinary

something to really note regarding artificial intelligence water use because it uses municipal water when municipal water in a cities water district gets used the rates for that watee go up thus the citizens of that city have to pay more money for their water without any commensurate impact to their quality of life as the data center doesnt actually provide any economic value to the city its in
